rationale.R1.1: Positioning requires correctly reconstructing QuPPL's gap statement and contribution claim — if the stated thesis is misdescribed, any comparison to prior work is built on a false premise.
rationale.R1.2: Each of the three contrast arguments relies on a logical inference (prior work lacks X, QuPPL provides X, therefore novel on X); those inferences must be internally consistent and non-circular.
rationale.R1.4: The delegation is precisely a novelty-assessment task; determining whether the delta claimed against general-purpose PPLs, calibration literature, and Bayesian semantics actually holds is the load-bearing obligation.
rationale.R1.7: Positioning claims live or die on whether the cited prior-work corpus is accurate, complete, and correctly attributed — missing a key reference in any of the three threads would undermine the distinctiveness argument.
